full_response:
In King Arthur’s inertial frame K the two starting lines are nailed to the ground and therefore have fixed spatial coordinates.  
Choose the origin of Arthur’s spatial axis to be midway between the two lines and let the +x-direction point from Knight #1 toward Knight #2.  
If the distance between the two starting lines is L, the line for Knight #1 is situated at

x_K = −L/2 .  

King Arthur defines the instant at which each knight crosses his own starting line to be the start of the race, so in Arthur’s frame those crossings are simultaneous and occur at his time t_K = 0.

Hence the space-time coordinates of event E₁ (Knight #1 crosses his starting line) in King Arthur’s frame are

\boxed{(t_K ,\, x_K)_{E_1} = \bigl(0,\,-L/2\bigr)}  

Confidence: 35%
